title: Acetosella setosa
taxonomy: Acetosella setosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Oxalidaceae. It belongs to the genus Acetosella, which is commonly known as the wood sorrel. The genus Acetosella is often considered a synonym of Oxalis, but some sources still recognize it as a distinct group. Acetosella setosa is native to Africa and Madagascar, and is known for its small, yellow or white flowers. This classification is subject to ongoing research and may be revised in the future.

intro: Acetosella setosa, commonly known as the sorrel or wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Oxalidaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, which include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, potentially aiding in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body. The bioactive constituents of Acetosella setosa include flavonoids, tannins, and oxalic acid, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Acetosella setosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce symptoms of arthritis.
